source_span:
CVE-2026-44228: Authentication Bypass in fastmiddleware

Published: 2026-06-02
CVSS 3.1 Base Score: 9.1 (Critical)
Affected versions: fastmiddleware v1.0.0 through v1.4.2
Fixed in: v1.5.0
what_changed: cvss-scorer classified fastmiddleware vulnerability as critical (9.1); advisory published to CVE database

source_span: When a request includes a malformed Authorization header with a truncated JWT, the middleware falls through to the next handler without rejecting the request.
what_changed: fastmiddleware token validation fails open on malformed JWT — request passes to next handler unauthenticated
uncertainty_note: Source and target unclear — the advisory describes a mechanism, not a specific event. Observer not identifiable from this span.

source_span: An unauthenticated attacker can craft requests that bypass all route-level authentication checks.
what_changed: attacker exploits authentication bypass to access protected routes

source_span:
Dependabot alert #387 — storefront-api
Severity: critical
Package: github.com/example/fastmiddleware
Vulnerable: v1.3.1 (pinned in go.mod)
Patched version: >= v1.5.0
what_changed: dependabot-bot translated CVE-2026-44228 into repo-specific alert #387 for storefront-api, classifying severity as critical

source_span: Picked up Dependabot #387. fastmiddleware auth bypass, CVSS 9.1. storefront-api uses this for all authenticated endpoints.
what_changed: on-call-engineer received dependabot alert and began triage of fastmiddleware vulnerability for storefront-api
uncertainty_note: Mediation unclear — did the engineer find the alert passively (email/dashboard) or was it actively pushed? Source text does not specify the delivery mechanism.

source_span: Blast radius: every route behind AuthRequired() middleware, which is basically the entire checkout flow and account management. Customer-facing. Not theoretical — this is exploitable over the network with a crafted header.
what_changed: on-call-engineer assessed blast radius as full checkout flow and account management; classified as customer-facing and practically exploitable

source_span: Holding off on a fix branch until we get sign-off on the upgrade path — v1.5.0 has a breaking change to the middleware signature so it's not a drop-in.
what_changed: fix branch blocked pending security sign-off; breaking API change in v1.5.0 prevents drop-in upgrade
uncertainty_note: Source attribution to semver-checker is inferred — the engineer notes the breaking change but does not name what tool identified it. Could be manual inspection.

source_span: Decision: Approve emergency hotfix. Risk accepted for breaking middleware signature change; regression coverage required before merge.
what_changed: security-lead approved emergency hotfix, accepting risk of breaking middleware signature change; required full regression coverage as gate condition

source_span: semver-checker flags v1.5.0 as minor-version bump with breaking API surface — this contradicts semver contract but the security severity overrides.
what_changed: security severity overrode semver-checker's breaking-change flag; semver contract violation accepted under security-critical policy

source_span: CI must pass with full integration suite before deployment.
what_changed: ci-pipeline required to pass full integration suite as deployment gate condition
uncertainty_note: This is a stated requirement, not a witnessed event. The CI run has not yet occurred at this point in the source material.

source_span: CI status: all 847 tests passing, 0 lint violations
what_changed: ci-runner executed full test suite (847 tests) and lint checks; all passed with zero violations

source_span: Canary: 5% traffic for 12 minutes, error rate 0.00%, p99 latency +2ms (within threshold)
what_changed: deployment-gate ran canary phase at 5% traffic for 12 minutes; error rate and latency within threshold parameters

source_span: Approver: Automated deployment gate (policy: security-critical-bypass). CI status: all 847 tests passing, 0 lint violations. Canary: 5% traffic for 12 minutes, error rate 0.00%, p99 latency +2ms (within threshold). Approved for full production rollout. 2026-06-02 17:45 UTC.
what_changed: automated deployment gate approved full production rollout under security-critical-bypass policy

source_span: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists in the token validation middleware of fastmiddleware.
what_changed: the vulnerability entered the storefront-api dependency chain and threatened customer data
